author: 0xunclebill
content:
claude thinks it might be a racy fcu call 


🤖 🤖 
Where Lodestar calls it — and the problem

It's issued from two unsynchronized paths:

1. After a payload envelope is revealed — importExecutionPayload.js:192-199 (step 7):
```
const head = this.forkChoice
if (!this.opts.disableImportExecutionFcU && blockRootHex === head.blockRoot) {
    ...
    this.executionEngine.notifyForkchoiceUpdate(fork, blockHashHex, safe, finalized)
        .catch((e) => { ... OT awaited
}
```

This is the call that should anchor Besu's txpool to the freshly-revealed parent payload. But it's (a) guarded by blockRootHex === head.blockRoot (skipped if that payload isn't the fork-choice head at import time — common in ePBS since payloads arrive late), and (b) fire-and-forget — not awaited, not ordered against anything.

2. At build time — produceBlockBody.js:508:
```
payloadId = await chain.execpdate(fork,toRootHex(parentBlockHash), safe, finalized, attributes);
```
This sets head=parentBlockHash and starts the build, then getPayload follows ~1s later.

Because these two FCUs aren't serialized, Besu's txpool head races. That's exactly what the EL showed: at build the txpool was anchored to 0x9546… (block #103, a sibling) while the build parent was 0x43a8… (#102) → inconsistent block header → the IL txs aren't executable against the wrong base state → Besu drops them → INCLUSION_LIST_UNSATISFIED. The constant prepType=Reorged in Lodestar's logs is the same symptom — its fcU head params keep changing under it.
